description: Test suite for ohos-issue-graphics-cppcrash-analysis skill - verifying that analysis results correctly identify crash root causes, address patterns, data flow origins, and thread competition issues from cppcrash faultlogs

evals.prompt: 分析这个cppcrash日志，定位崩溃根因
evals.expected_output: Should identify fault_addr 0x6b6b92503c687c50 as Use-After-Free pattern (0x6b6b prefix = jemalloc freed-memory marker), match x8 and x20 registers containing 0x6b6b values as UAF evidence, corroborate from memory near x20 showing 6b6b6b6b pattern, trace data flow to find which thread freed the object, determine root cause as accessing a freed object without synchronization

assertions.evidence: Correctly identifies 0x6b6b prefix in fault_addr as Use-After-Free pattern per SKILL.md and memory-pattern-analysis.md
Explains 0x6b6b is jemalloc freed-memory marker, not random corruption
Corroborates UAF conclusion from both register values (x8, x20) and memory near x20 showing 6b6b6b6b6b6b6b6b pattern
Traces register assignment chain to find where 0x6b6b value enters, identifies which thread freed the object per memory-pattern-analysis.md
Determines root cause: object freed by another thread/lifecycle, then accessed by crashing thread without synchronization

evals.prompt: 分析这个cppcrash日志，定位崩溃根因
evals.expected_output: Should identify fault_addr 0x0000000000000010 as null pointer + offset pattern (base=0 + offset=0x10), verify x0=0 and x20=0 confirming null pointer dereference, resolve #00 pc via llvm-addr2line to find exact code line and identify which member at offset 0x10 was accessed, trace why pointer was null

assertions.evidence: Correctly identifies fault_addr=0x10 as null pointer + offset pattern (base address 0 + member offset 0x10)
Verifies x0=0 and x20=0 confirm null pointer, not just 'close to zero'
Does NOT falsely claim UAF (0x6b6b pattern not present) per SKILL.md forbidden rules
Determines offset 0x10 as specific member access on null object pointer via source code analysis
Root cause: pointer was null (uninitialized, reset, or returned null from lookup) and accessed without null check

evals.prompt: 分析这个cppcrash日志，定位崩溃根因
evals.expected_output: Should identify SEGV_ACCERR as access permission violation, distinguish from SEGV_MAPERR, verify fault_addr 0x59810e9658 exists in Maps as rw-p region, analyze whether this is genuine permission mismatch or memory corruption causing wrong access type on a valid address

assertions.evidence: Correctly distinguishes SEGV_ACCERR (access permission error) from SEGV_MAPERR (no mapping) per SKILL.md
Verifies fault_addr 0x59810e9658 falls in Maps range 0x5981000000-0x59810f0000 (rw-p) per memory-data-analysis.md
Analyzes what type of access (read/write/execute) the crash instruction was attempting
Evaluates whether corruption changed pointer/vtable causing wrong access, or genuine permission mismatch
Determines root cause with evidence: either genuine permission issue or corruption-induced wrong access

evals.prompt: 分析这个cppcrash日志，定位崩溃根因
evals.expected_output: Should identify SIGABRT with abort() as active termination (not memory access error), trace from abort+48 through #01/#02 to find business code that triggered abort, identify the assertion/check that failed, analyze whether the failure is genuine logic error or corruption-induced

assertions.evidence: Correctly identifies SIGABRT as active termination, not memory access error per SKILL.md
Traces from abort+48 back through #01/#02 to find business code that called abort per special-cases.md and call-chain-backtrace.md
Identifies what specific assertion/check in business code failed and triggered the abort
Analyzes whether condition violation is genuine logic error or corruption-induced false failure
Root cause: specific business condition violated triggering abort, with evidence supporting conclusion

evals.prompt: 分析这个cppcrash日志，定位崩溃根因
evals.expected_output: Should identify terminate caused by uncaught std::bad_cast exception (from LastFatalMessage), trace which dynamic_cast threw bad_cast, evaluate per special-cases.md rule #2 whether bad_cast is genuine type error OR memory corruption damaging vtable/typeinfo, check memory near registers for corruption evidence (x19+16 shows trailing zeros), determine root cause with certainty level

assertions.evidence: Correctly identifies this as terminate from uncaught exception, not simple abort() call per special-cases.md
Identifies exception type as std::bad_cast from LastFatalMessage
Traces which dynamic_cast in business code threw the bad_cast per call-chain-backtrace.md
Evaluates whether bad_cast is genuine type mismatch OR memory corruption damaging vtable/typeinfo per special-cases.md rule #2, checks memory near registers for corruption evidence
Determines root cause with certainty level (confirmed vs inferred): genuine cast error or corruption-induced type confusion

evals.prompt: 分析这个cppcrash日志，定位崩溃根因
evals.expected_output: Should recognize cfi_slowpath_comm in stack as CFI failure indicating memory corruption per special-cases.md, prioritize memory stomping analysis path per SKILL.md execution strategy, identify 0xdeadbeef in x19+16 memory dump as corruption marker, analyze whether vtable/typeinfo was damaged causing CFI failure, trace what stomped the object metadata

assertions.evidence: Recognizes cfi_slowpath_comm as CFI failure indicating memory corruption per special-cases.md rule #3
Prioritizes memory stomping/corruption analysis path per SKILL.md execution strategy table row for cfi_slowpath_comm
Identifies 0xdeadbeef in x19+16 memory dump as corruption/stomping marker per memory-pattern-analysis.md special values
Analyzes whether corruption damaged vtable pointer or typeinfo causing CFI type check failure
Traces which thread/code wrote corrupted data to the object metadata per thread-competition-analysis.md and memory-pattern-analysis.md
Root cause: memory corruption stomped object vtable/typeinfo, causing CFI failure on type check

evals.prompt: 分析这个cppcrash日志，定位崩溃根因
evals.expected_output: Should analyze Other thread info showing RSUniRenderThread calling RSRenderNodeDrawableCache::GetDrawableById() while crashing thread accessing same cache, identify concurrent access to shared DrawableCache, match race condition pattern: one thread iterating while another modifying, determine iterator invalidation or concurrent read-write without lock as root cause

assertions.evidence: Analyzes Other thread info for concurrent access patterns per thread-competition-analysis.md
Identifies RSRenderNodeDrawableCache as shared data structure accessed by both crashing thread and RSUniRenderThread
Matches race condition pattern: thread iterating while another calls GetDrawableById modifying same cache per thread-competition-analysis.md rule #4
Determines specific crash mechanism: iterator invalidation or concurrent read-write without lock
Root cause: race condition on shared DrawableCache between threads without proper synchronization, with specific fix suggestion

evals.prompt: 分析这个cppcrash日志，定位崩溃根因
evals.expected_output: Should match address pattern: fault_addr 0x09810e9658 vs legitimate addresses x0=0x59810e9658 and x19=0x59810e9658, identify high byte corruption (0x59->0x09), verify via Maps that 0x59810e9658 is valid but 0x09810e9658 is not, trace stomping source and which thread caused the overflow

assertions.evidence: Identifies high byte difference: 0x59->0x09 between fault_addr and legitimate addresses via byte-level comparison per memory-pattern-analysis.md
Verifies via Maps: 0x59810e9658 falls in valid range 0x5981000000-0x59810f0000 but 0x09810e9658 is not mapped per memory-data-analysis.md
Matches high byte stomping pattern per memory-pattern-analysis.md: '高位字节被修改：0x59xxxxxx -> 0x09xxxxxx'
Traces what out-of-bounds write stomped the high byte, identifies which thread caused the overflow per thread-competition-analysis.md
Root cause: adjacent buffer overflow or out-of-bounds write corrupted high byte of object pointer

evals.prompt: 分析这个cppcrash日志，定位崩溃根因
evals.expected_output: Should recognize jemalloc_mallctl at stack top as memory debugging/protection mechanism per special-cases.md rule #4, identify 0xdeadbeef in x19+16 memory dump as corruption marker, analyze business code in #02/#03 that triggered jemalloc abort, determine whether 0xdeadbeef is debug marker or actual stomping data, trace adjacent allocation overflow as stomping source

assertions.evidence: Recognizes jemalloc_mallctl at stack top as memory debugging/protection abort per special-cases.md rule #4
Identifies 0xdeadbeef in x19+16 memory dump as corruption/stomping marker per memory-pattern-analysis.md
Determines whether 0xdeadbeef is jemalloc debug marker or actual corruption data stomping the object
Analyzes business code at #02/#03 for buffer overrun causing stomping detected by jemalloc
Root cause: memory corruption/stomping detected by jemalloc protection mechanism, traced to specific buffer overrun

evals.prompt: 分析这个cppcrash日志，快速给我根因
evals.expected_output: Should NOT guess root cause without evidence or use vague expressions like '可能是'/'也许是'. Must verify fault_addr 0x6b6b pattern before claiming UAF, must check Other thread info before concluding single-thread cause, must trace data flow to free point. Conclusions must be evidence-based per SKILL.md forbidden rules

assertions.evidence: Avoids vague expressions like '可能是' or '也许是' per SKILL.md forbidden rule #1
Verifies fault_addr 0x6b6b pattern before claiming UAF per SKILL.md: '看到0x6b6b地址就断定是UAF → 必须追溯数据流'
Checks Other thread info before concluding per SKILL.md: '禁止只分析崩溃线程而忽略其它线程'
Traces data flow from registers to find free point before concluding UAF per SKILL.md
All conclusions backed by specific evidence (register values, memory patterns, thread context)

evals.prompt: 分析这个cppcrash日志
evals.expected_output: Should NOT assume SIGSEGV means null pointer without verifying fault_addr. For this file fault_addr=0x6b6b92503c687c50, clearly NOT null pointer. Must identify as UAF (0x6b6b prefix) instead. Must verify fault_addr characteristics before claiming any crash type per SKILL.md forbidden rule

assertions.evidence: Verifies fault_addr=0x6b6b92503c687c50 is NOT zero, so NOT null pointer per SKILL.md: '看到SIGSEGV就断定是空指针 → 必须先验证fault_addr'
Correctly identifies as UAF based on 0x6b6b address pattern, not null pointer
Does NOT shortcut to 'null pointer' conclusion just because signal is SIGSEGV

evals.prompt: 分析这个cppcrash日志
evals.expected_output: Must NOT skip address pattern matching just because address appears in Maps. Must compare crash address with legitimate addresses byte-by-byte. For this file fault_addr differs from legitimate addresses in high byte (0x59->0x09). This subtle difference must be identified as stomping evidence per SKILL.md forbidden rule

assertions.evidence: Performs byte-level comparison between fault_addr and legitimate addresses per SKILL.md: '禁止不对比崩溃地址与合法地址的差异'
Does NOT miss the subtle high byte difference per SKILL.md: '忽略了高位字节被踩的细微差异（如0x59→0x09）'
Identifies stomping from address pattern matching, not just Maps range check
Calculates actual access address from crash instruction + registers to verify pattern per SKILL.md

evals.prompt: 分析这个cppcrash日志
evals.expected_output: Must NOT only analyze crashing thread and ignore other threads. Must check Other thread info for concurrent access. For this file: RSUniRenderThread calling GetDrawableById on same cache is essential evidence for race condition. Ignoring it would miss the root cause per SKILL.md forbidden rule #3

assertions.evidence: Analyzes Other thread info, not just crashing thread per SKILL.md: '禁止只分析崩溃线程而忽略其它线程'
Identifies shared data structure (DrawableCache) accessed by both crashing and other threads
Does NOT miss race condition evidence from other threads per SKILL.md: '禁止忽略锁竞争和竞态条件'
Root cause involves multiple threads, not just crashing thread code

evals.prompt: 分析这个cppcrash日志
evals.expected_output: When evidence does not clearly point to a single root cause, must list all possible root causes with supporting evidence per SKILL.md. Must mark which conclusions are confirmed vs inferred. Must describe further analysis directions needed. Must NOT force a single conclusion when evidence supports multiple possibilities

assertions.evidence: Lists multiple possible root causes (memory corruption, type confusion, race condition) when single cause uncertain per SKILL.md
Clearly marks which conclusions are confirmed by evidence and which are inferred per SKILL.md: '明确标注哪些结论是确定的，哪些是推断的'
Describes directions for further analysis to resolve ambiguity per SKILL.md: '说明需要进一步分析的方向'
Does NOT force a single root cause conclusion when evidence supports multiple possibilities

evals.prompt: 分析这个cppcrash日志
evals.expected_output: Should verify buildId match between cppcrash and so file per SKILL.md precheck step #3. If mismatch detected, must terminate analysis and report user that version mismatch prevents accurate stack unwinding. Must NOT proceed with wrong so producing incorrect line numbers per SKILL.md forbidden rule #2

assertions.evidence: Verifies buildId match between cppcrash and so file per SKILL.md precheck step #3
If mismatch, terminates analysis per SKILL.md: '若buildId不匹配 → 报告用户版本不一致，终止分析'
Does NOT proceed with stack unwinding using mismatched so per SKILL.md: '禁止使用buildId不匹配的so解栈'

evals.prompt: 分析这个cppcrash日志
evals.expected_output: Should detect missing so in lib.unstripped/ per SKILL.md precheck, report user that symbol table file is needed for accurate line number resolution, explain analysis is limited to function names in cppcrash without precise line numbers, mark in report that stack unwinding is incomplete per SKILL.md fallback

assertions.evidence: Detects missing so file in lib.unstripped/ directory per SKILL.md precheck
Reports to user that correct symbol table file is needed per SKILL.md: '若无法找到对应so → 报告用户需要提供正确的符号表文件'
Explains analysis limited to function names in cppcrash, without precise line numbers
Marks in report that stack unwinding could not be completed per SKILL.md fallback

evals.prompt: 分析这个cppcrash日志
evals.expected_output: Should detect missing source code in code/ directory, provide analysis from available cppcrash data (registers, memory, maps, fault_addr), explain that call chain analysis and code review cannot be completed without source, mark in report which analysis steps are limited per SKILL.md fallback

assertions.evidence: Detects missing source code in code/ directory per SKILL.md precheck
Provides analysis from available cppcrash data (registers, memory, maps, fault_addr) per SKILL.md
Marks in report which analysis steps (code review, call chain, lifecycle) are limited per SKILL.md
States that providing source code would enable deeper root cause analysis

evals.prompt: 分析这个cppcrash日志，定位崩溃根因
evals.expected_output: Report must contain per report-template.md: metadata summary, call stack analysis, code location with exact file:line, disassembly analysis, data flow analysis, call chain backtrace, thread competition analysis, memory data analysis, address pattern matching, root cause inference, fix suggestions. Must avoid vague expressions, must include evidence-based conclusions

assertions.evidence: Report includes device info, module, signal type, fault_addr per report-template.md section 1
Report includes exact file path and line number per report-template.md section 3
Report includes address pattern matching analysis per report-template.md section 9
Report includes evidence-based root cause per report-template.md section 10
Report includes specific fix suggestions per report-template.md section 11
Report avoids vague expressions like '可能是' per report-template.md quality requirements

evals.prompt: 分析这个cppcrash日志，定位崩溃根因
evals.expected_output: Root cause inference must be rigorous per report-template.md: data flow traced to find register anomaly origin, address pattern matching done to identify stomping characteristics, precise code line located, call chain and parameter source fully traced, multiple possibilities considered, if evidence insufficient explicitly state uncertainty

assertions.evidence: Root cause backed by complete evidence chain: address pattern -> data flow -> stomping source
Data flow traced to find where anomalous value was introduced per report-template.md rigor
Address pattern matching identifies stomping characteristics per report-template.md rigor
Precise code line number located per report-template.md rigor
If any conclusion is uncertain, explicitly states uncertainty level per report-template.md rigor

evals.prompt: 分析这个cppcrash日志，定位崩溃根因
evals.expected_output: Should use llvm-objdump to disassemble crash point, identify exact crash instruction, calculate actual access address from instruction operands + register values, verify calculated address matches fault_addr in Reason field. Should use llvm-addr2line to resolve code line numbers. Should verify buildId before unwinding

assertions.evidence: Identifies exact crash instruction via llvm-objdump disassembly per disassembly-analysis.md
Calculates actual access address from instruction operands + register values per disassembly-analysis.md
Verifies calculated address matches fault_addr in Reason field per disassembly-analysis.md
Verifies buildId match before using llvm-addr2line per stack-unwinding.md
Resolves exact code line via llvm-addr2line per stack-unwinding.md

evals.prompt: 分析这个cppcrash日志，定位崩溃根因
evals.expected_output: Should trace call chain from crash point upward per call-chain-backtrace.md: analyze each call layer's function signature and parameter passing, identify where problematic pointer/object entered the chain, analyze object lifecycle (when created, potentially freed, still valid at crash), determine whether another thread modified/freed the object during execution

assertions.evidence: Analyzes each call layer (#00, #01, #02) for function signature and parameter passing per call-chain-backtrace.md
Identifies where the problematic pointer/object entered the call chain per call-chain-backtrace.md
Analyzes object lifecycle: when created, when potentially freed, whether still valid at crash point per call-chain-backtrace.md
Identifies whether another thread modified/freed the object during call chain execution per call-chain-backtrace.md

evals.prompt: 分析这个cppcrash日志，定位崩溃根因
evals.expected_output: Should analyze Memory near registers data per memory-data-analysis.md: identify memory layout around this pointer, recognize anomaly patterns (0xdeadbeef as corruption marker, 0x6b6b as freed fill, byte-level stomping), check which object field boundary anomaly falls at, compare anomaly addresses with Maps ranges, explain pattern meaning

assertions.evidence: Analyzes memory layout around this pointer per memory-data-analysis.md rule #1
Identifies 0xdeadbeef as anomaly pattern per memory-data-analysis.md rule #2
Checks which object field boundary the anomaly falls at per memory-data-analysis.md
Compares anomaly addresses with Maps ranges per memory-data-analysis.md rule #3
Explains the meaning of identified pattern (debug marker, stomping data, freed fill) per memory-pattern-analysis.md

evals.prompt: 分析这个cppcrash日志，定位崩溃根因
evals.expected_output: Should use code-review-checklist.md to check located code for: mutex usage correctness, lock scope sufficiency, iterator invalidation during concurrent modification, shared_ptr lifecycle across threads. Must identify specific code patterns causing the race condition (e.g., missing lock during iteration, insufficient lock scope)

assertions.evidence: Checks mutex usage correctness per code-review-checklist.md rule #1
Analyzes whether lock scope covers entire critical section including iteration per code-review-checklist.md
Checks for iterator invalidation during concurrent container modification per code-review-checklist.md
Checks shared_ptr lifecycle management across threads per code-review-checklist.md rule #5
Identifies specific code pattern (e.g., missing lock during iteration) causing the race condition
